properties.description: Shanghai's busiest shopping street — 5.5km of malls, department stores, and street vendors
properties.notes: The east end connects to The Bund. Very crowded on evenings and weekends.

properties.description: Maze of narrow alleyways (longtang) in the French Concession converted into an artisan market with independent boutiques, studios, cafes, and galleries
properties.sustainability_notes: Adaptive reuse of historic lilong (lane) housing; supports independent local artisans and small businesses
properties.notes: Best for browsing unique Shanghai-themed art prints, ceramics, and gifts. Some resident artists still live and work on upper floors. Enter via Taikang Rd or Ruijin 2nd Rd.

properties.description: Upscale pedestrian entertainment district built from restored 1920s Shikumen stone-gate houses, now home to flagship restaurants, international boutiques, and galleries
properties.address: Taicang Rd / Madang Rd, Huangpu District
properties.sustainability_notes: Historic Shikumen architecture preserved and adaptively reused rather than demolished
properties.notes: North block is more commercial (shops/restaurants); South block is quieter with galleries and the Shikumen Open House Museum (20 CNY). Good for evening dining and drinks. Walking distance to Tianzifang.

properties.name_cn: 亚太新阳服装礼品市场
properties.description: Massive underground market near Science & Technology Museum metro station, famous among expats for tailored suits, electronics, bags, and souvenirs at negotiable prices
properties.address: Near Science & Technology Museum Metro Station, Century Ave, Pudong
properties.notes: Bargaining is expected and necessary — start at 10-20% of the asking price. Best for electronics accessories, tailor-made clothing, and luggage. The adjacent Science & Technology Museum is worth a visit if you have kids.

properties.description: Traditional-style bazaar surrounding Yu Garden with hundreds of shops selling Chinese handicrafts, silk, jade, antiques, tea, and souvenir items
properties.notes: Prices are tourist-level but the setting is picturesque. Good for Chinese tea sets, silk scarves, and traditional snacks as gifts. The zigzag bridge over the koi pond is a classic Shanghai photo spot.

properties.description: Former textile mill on Suzhou Creek converted into Shanghai's premier contemporary art district, housing over 100 galleries, studios, and creative businesses
properties.sustainability_notes: Industrial heritage building adaptively reused; supports emerging Chinese contemporary artists
properties.notes: Free to enter the complex. Individual galleries are free or charge a small fee. Best on weekends when more studios are open. The building facades are covered in murals and street art. Good cafe options inside.
